message:
feat(stdio/vis): port svis(3) extra-bytes family (bd-igo1n)

Adds the NetBSD libutil svis(3) extra-bytes variants of the
already-shipped vis(3) family. Each variant takes an extra
NUL-terminated `extra` argument; bytes appearing in `extra` are
forced to escape even when they would otherwise pass through (e.g.
printable ASCII).

Six new C entry points:

- svis(dst, c, flags, nextc, extra)
- snvis(dst, dlen, c, flags, nextc, extra)
- strsvis(dst, src, flags, extra)
- strsnvis(dst, dlen, src, flags, extra)
- strsvisx(dst, src, srclen, flags, extra)
- strsnvisx(dst, dlen, src, srclen, flags, extra)

Core additions:

- encode_byte_with_extra: extends encode_byte by force-escaping
  printable bytes listed in extra (default → caret form, VIS_OCTAL
  → octal triple). '\\' is unaffected (always doubled).
- strvis_to_vec_with_extra: bulk variant.

ABI shims share an `extras_slice` helper that returns an empty slice
for NULL `extra` (so passing NULL degrades cleanly to the non-extras
behavior). Bounded variants return NULL/-1 on overflow without
writing anything.

17 integration tests cover: passthrough vs forced-escape, octal
flag interaction, NULL-extras = identity to non-extras sibling,
NULL-dst/NULL-src guards, bounded overflow, exact-room success,
embedded-NUL handling via strsvisx, full strsvis→strunvis round
trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-fnm-epic","title":"PORT: fnmatch.h native Rust impl in frankenlibc-core","description":"fnmatch lives entirely in frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs (~280 lines: classify_bracket + fnmatch_impl + pub fnmatch wrapper) with all pattern-matching logic inlined alongside the C ABI surface. Pure pattern matching with no syscalls — natural fit for a safe-Rust core port. The port enables independent unit testing of the pattern engine, surfaces the full POSIX surface (literal chars, ?, *, [...], ranges, negation, FNM_PATHNAME, FNM_NOESCAPE, FNM_PERIOD, FNM_CASEFOLD GNU ext), and opens the door for property-based testing. Sub-beads: bd-fnm-1 core engine on byte slices, bd-fnm-2 wire fl::fnmatch to delegate, bd-fnm-3 conformance + GNU FNM_LEADING_DIR / FNM_FILE_NAME parity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-24T23:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T00:30:00Z","close_reason":"Epic complete. All 3 sub-beads closed: bd-fnm-1 (frankenlibc-core/src/string/fnmatch.rs with FnmatchFlags + fnmatch_match() + classify_bracket + fnmatch_inner, 16 unit tests), bd-fnm-2 (replaced ~280 lines of inline fnmatch_impl in fl-abi with 14-line shim delegating to core), bd-fnm-3 (4 conformance regression tests for FNM_LEADING_DIR + FNM_FILE_NAME alias + property tests). Total: 16 core unit tests + 7 conformance tests = 23 tests for the family, 0 divergences against glibc. The fnmatch engine is now native, unit-testable independent of the FFI surface, and uses safe slice-based pure Rust.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","fnmatch","native-rust","porting"]}
 {"id":"bd-frlk1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc fcntl record-locks (F_SETLK/F_GETLK) diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). Tests fcntl with F_SETLK + F_WRLCK (write lock + unlock round-trip), F_RDLCK (read lock), F_GETLK on unlocked region returns F_UNLCK, F_SETLK with NULL flock pointer fails on both. Complements bd-flk1 flock+lockf coverage with byte-range record locks. 5 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T16:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","fcntl","record-locks"]}
-{"id":"bd-ftchp","title":"PORT: unvis (NetBSD vis(3) streaming byte decoder)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) streaming decoder unvis(*cp, c, *astate, flag) — single-byte state-machine inverse of vis(3). Caller feeds bytes one at a time; the function returns UNVIS_VALID (1) when *cp holds a fully decoded byte, UNVIS_VALIDPUSH (2) when *cp is decoded AND the current input byte should be re-fed, UNVIS_NOCHAR (3) for partial sequences, UNVIS_SYNBAD (-1) for malformed input. The opaque *astate carries decoder state across calls. Used by mtree, BSD pkg tools, and anything needing a streaming decoder rather than the all-at-once strunvis. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdio::vis::UnvisDecoder state machine + thin abi shim that maps the i32 state cell to/from the typed enum.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:10:29.760546150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:10:29.760546150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

The opaque *astate carries decoder state across calls. Used by mtree, BSD pkg tools, and anything needing a streaming decoder rather than the all-at-once strunvis.  {"id":"bd-ftw-2","title":"PORT ftw.h #2: closure-based walker driver in core","description":"Add WalkContext + walk_tree() function operating via closures: lstat/stat/readdir/is_dir/is_symlink/dev_id. Implements pre-order vs post-order (FTW_DEPTH), symlink handling (FTW_PHYS), filesystem boundary check (FTW_MOUNT), and POSIX type-flag dispatch. Pure-safe Rust (no syscalls in core). Unit tests use a mock FsOps implementation. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T01:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-25T01:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T01:45:00Z","close_reason":"Created crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ftw/walker.rs with: trait StatLike (is_dir/is_symlink/dev_id), trait FsOps (associated Stat: StatLike, stat/lstat/read_dir methods), pub fn walk_tree(root, fs, flags, visit) returning -1 on root-stat fail / first-non-zero visit / 0. Pure-safe Rust. Implements pre-order vs post-order (FTW_DEPTH), symlink classification under FTW_PHYS (Symlink vs DanglingSymlink), filesystem boundary (FTW_MOUNT), DirNoRead on read_dir failure, StatFailed on stat failure (with default-valued stat per POSIX undefined-contents semantics). 7 walker unit tests pass via in-memory MockFs (visits_all_entries, nonexistent_root_returns_minus_one, callback_nonzero_short_circuits, depth_visits_dirs_post_order, mount_skips_other_filesystem, phys_distinguishes_symlinks, unreadable_dir_reports_dirnoread). Total 20 core ftw tests (13 path + 7 walker). fl-abi rebuilds clean. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ftw","porting","walker"]}

Concrete fs-ops closures: lstat via syscall::sys_newfstatat (with AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW), readdir via crate::dirent_abi opendir/readdir/closedir. Public ABI signatures unchanged. Verify all conformance tests still pass. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T01:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-25T02:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T02:00:00Z","close_reason":"Replaced ~339 lines of inline ftw_walk_dir + nftw + nftw_walk_dir + FtwInfo struct in unistd_abi.rs with a thin shim layer (~155 lines): AbiStat(libc::stat) wrapper implementing core's StatLike trait via st_mode/st_dev field reads + manual Default impl using mem::zeroed (since libc::stat doesn't impl Default), AbiFs FsOps impl wiring sys_newfstatat (with/without AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW) for stat/lstat and crate::dirent_abi opendir/readdir/closedir for read_dir, ftw + nftw shims that build &[u8] from the C string, call frankenlibc_core::ftw::walk_tree, and bridge the visit closure back to the user's C callback (NUL-terminating each path on the way out + building FtwInfo struct for nftw). Public ABI signatures unchanged. The bd-ftw2 fix (nonexistent root → -1) is preserved by core's walk_tree probe. All 6 ftw conformance tests pass: ftw_diff_coverage_report, diff_ftw_nonexistent_dir, diff_ftw_visits_same_set, diff_nftw_depth_visits_same_set, diff_nftw_phys_visits_same_set, diff_nftw_nonexistent_dir. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","ftw","porting"]}

 {"id":"bd-id3","title":"Verification matrix: required unit/e2e/log artifacts for every critique bead","description":"Goal: make verification obligations explicit in plan space before implementation.

Deliverables:
- Machine-readable matrix keyed by bead ID with required evidence fields:
  - unit tests (commands + expected assertions)
  - e2e scripts (strict/hardened coverage)
  - structured log artifacts (schema + sample)
  - perf/isomorphism evidence where applicable
- Coverage status dashboard (missing/partial/complete).

Acceptance:
- Every open critique bead has a populated verification row.
- Missing evidence blocks bead closure.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:39:53.468918289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:00:59.835959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:00:59.835959Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","logging","testing","unit","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-1s7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-34w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-38s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-3n0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1100,"issue_id":"bd-id3","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Machine-readable verification matrix: tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json
- Covers all 57 open/in_progress critique beads
- Each bead mapped to 6 obligation types: unit_tests, e2e_scripts, structured_logs, perf_evidence, conformance_fixtures, golden_artifacts
- Coverage status per obligation: complete | partial | missing | not_required
- Dashboard with summary stats by coverage status, priority, and obligation type
- Artifact catalog linking to existing test infrastructure

### 2. CI validation script: scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh
Four checks:
1. Matrix file exists and is valid JSON
2. Schema structure validation (top-level keys, entry fields, count consistency)
3. All critique beads have verification rows (cross-checked against .beads/issues.jsonl)
4. Coverage gap report (informational dashboard)

### 3. Integration tests: crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/verification_matrix_test.rs
7 tests:
- matrix_exists_and_valid_json
- schema_defines_required_types
- all_critique_beads_have_rows
- entry_coverage_counts_consistent
- dashboard_stats_consistent_with_entries
- entries_have_valid_coverage_statuses
- no_empty_bead_ids

### Test commands

running 7 tests

test schema_defines_required_types ... ok
test entry_coverage_counts_consistent ... ok
test dashboard_stats_consistent_with_entries ... ok

test entries_have_valid_coverage_statuses ... ok

 {"id":"bd-ie8zc","title":"atoi clamps to INT_MAX on overflow instead of truncating per POSIX (int)strtol equivalence","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/conversion.rs\` atoi() clamps
the strtol result into i32 range:

    if val > i32::MAX as i64 { i32::MAX }
    else if val < i32::MIN as i64 { i32::MIN }
    else { val as i32 }

POSIX SUSv4 specifies that atoi(str) is equivalent to
\`(int) strtol(str, (char **)NULL, 10)\`. \`(int) long_val\` on
2's-complement machines (all targets we support) is a TRUNCATION
cast, not a saturating clamp.

Observed divergence (found by fuzz_stdlib):
- Input: \"99999999999999\" (fourteen 9s)
- strtol → 99999999999999 (fits in i64, no overflow, errno unchanged)
- glibc atoi: (int) 99999999999999 = 0x107A3FFF = 276447231
- Our atoi: clamps, returns INT_MAX = 2147483647

## Fix

Replace the if-else clamp with a bare \`val as i32\` cast. C
standard says overflow behavior is UB; POSIX equivalence with
(int)strtol pins it down to truncation on 2's-complement.
matches glibc's observable behavior.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P2). Any C program that relies on atoi's documented
POSIX equivalence to \`(int)strtol\` gets a different value from
us than from glibc when the source string overflows i32. Likely
rare in production (most callers already bound their input), but
a documented divergence from glibc.","notes":"

Fixed in 6d29fb13: atoi returns `val as i32` (truncation cast) instead of clamping to INT_MAX, matching POSIX SUSv4 equivalence with (int)strtol. Verified: 60s fuzz_stdlib clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:01:52.755209221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:14:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:14:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ifrq","title":"Conformance: direct execute inet_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for inet_ops.json cases in the harness alongside existing isolated harness coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:24:36.343615360Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:31:36.967633158Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:31:36.965056032Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for inet_ops fixtures and verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","inet","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-igo1n","title":"PORT: vis(3) extra-bytes family (svis, snvis, strsvis, strsnvis, strsvisx, strsnvisx)","description":"Port the NetBSD vis(3) extra-bytes family: svis(dst,c,flags,nextc,extra), snvis(dst,dlen,c,flags,nextc,extra), strsvis(dst,src,flags,extra), strsnvis(dst,dlen,src,flags,extra), strsvisx(dst,src,srclen,flags,extra), strsnvisx(dst,dlen,src,srclen,flags,extra). These are siblings of the already-shipped vis/nvis/strvis/strnvis/strvisx/strnvisx that additionally treat any byte present in the NUL-terminated extra string as needing escape, even if it would otherwise be a printable passthrough. Implementation: extend core encode_byte with an extras predicate; add thin abi shims; integration tests for extras-trigger-escape, NULL extras = identity to non-extra variant, bounded overflow guards.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:54:16.397672283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:54:16.397672283Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ihl3","title":"Conformance: add execute_* handlers for dirent_ops family (3 symbols)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:24:38.056550633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:27:27.621930699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:27:27.619986984Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-ijybx","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] pidfd_spawn uses posix_spawn + pidfd_open — PID-reuse race defeats pidfd guarantees","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs:5573 pidfd_spawn implements via posix_spawn + pidfd_open(child_pid, 0). Between those two calls the child may exit and the PID be recycled, so pidfd_open resolves to the wrong process. This defeats the entire point of pidfd (the race-free reference-a-process primitive). glibc 2.39's pidfd_spawn uses clone3 with CLONE_PIDFD atomically; our CLONE_PIDFD support already exists (frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs:375). Fix direction: re-implement pidfd_spawn/pidfd_spawnp via clone3(CLONE_PIDFD) + exec, OR at minimum block SIGCHLD across the window and check the child's start time on pidfd_open. Reach: any LD_PRELOAD'd app that calls pidfd_spawn on short-lived children (common in container orchestration) may signal the wrong process. Same issue in pidfd_spawnp line 5603.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:50:02.842113919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T01:00:27.928041413Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T01:00:27.926118812Z","close_reason":"Reworked pidfd_spawn/pidfd_spawnp to use clone3(CLONE_PIDFD) and added pidfd spawn regressions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ily44","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] iconv() TOCTOU use-after-free with concurrent iconv_close()","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/iconv_abi.rs lines 176-188, the iconv() function validates the descriptor handle via is_known_handle(cd) (HashSet lookup), then dereferences via 'unsafe { &mut *cd.cast::<IconvDescriptor>() }'. There is no synchronization between the lookup and the dereference.

If thread B calls iconv_close(cd) between thread A's is_known_handle check and the &mut * dereference, the descriptor's Box is freed (line 350: 'unsafe { Box::from_raw(cd.cast::<IconvDescriptor>()) }'), and thread A then dereferences freed memory --- use-after-free.

POSIX does not explicitly forbid concurrent iconv()/iconv_close() on the same descriptor, but a robust libc should not crash with UAF. Mitigation options:

1. Hold the handles() Mutex for the duration of iconv() (cheap, but serializes all conversions across descriptors).
2. Replace the HashSet<usize> registry with HashMap<usize, Arc<Mutex<IconvDescriptor>>> so iconv() takes an Arc clone before releasing the registry lock; iconv_close removes the registry entry and drops one Arc reference, but the active iconv() call's Arc keeps the descriptor alive until it returns.
3. Use a generational handle scheme (sequence counter + slot index) to detect stale handles.

Option 2 is recommended: per-descriptor mutex avoids serialization across descriptors, and Arc reference-counting cleanly handles the lifetime issue.

Severity: HIGH — UAF in interposed libc function under multi-threaded use.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:52:59.789930171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: commit c8003957 (peer agent) refactored iconv registry to Arc<Mutex<IconvDescriptor>>; concurrent close + iconv no longer UAFs. 41/41 iconv tests pass including the new TOCTOU regression.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
